summary: Current-code and active external-report planning review closed the external-report archive decision and recorded or automated CLI-surface, source-package lane, structural-budget, and report-provenance follow-ups.

observations.problem: The 2026-05-13 crosscheck improvement report was initially present as a root active external report while generated-artifact-index classified it as an archive candidate covered by the broader 2026-05-11 active report.
observations.why_it_matters: Long-term improvement planning can drift from machine-readable release evidence if active external reports are not folded into the reference/action lifecycle before closeout.
observations.automation_candidate: Automated by adding reference manifest alignment to external-report-action-matrix, adding checked-in lifecycle static tests, applying the generated archive execution manifest for the 2026-05-13 report, and refreshing the strict reference manifest, action matrix, generated artifact index, and artifact freshness report.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. The active external report root now contains only report-reference-manifest after archiving the covered 2026-05-11 and 2026-05-13 reports; generated-artifact-index reports archive_candidate_count=0, report-reference-manifest has active_reference_set_status=current after the settle pass, and make external-report-reference-manifest-settle is now wired into release-evidence-closeout and external-report-lifecycle-refresh so active/archive moves converge to a checked-in current manifest before downstream action-matrix evidence. Future root external report additions fail static lifecycle coverage when the checked-in manifest or action matrix omits the active report set.

observations.surface: .github/workflows/ci.yml, ops/scripts/__init__.py, tests/test_import_fallback_contract.py, pyproject.toml project.scripts, mk/*.mk flat python -m calls
observations.problem: The current test contract checks flat alias --help only with the active interpreter, while CI covers Python 3.12 and 3.14 but not Python 3.13 even though the external report documented a Python 3.13 flat alias failure mode.
observations.why_it_matters: A supported minor-version execution surface can regress silently when Makefile targets continue to rely on flat ops.scripts aliases and the cross-version matrix skips the affected interpreter.
observations.automation_candidate: Partially automated by adding Python 3.13 to the CI matrix and adding a CLI surface inventory test for project scripts, Makefile flat module calls, and direct fallback entrypoints.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed for CI coverage and local inventory. A future hardening slice can replace proxy flat aliases with wrapper files or canonical module paths if Python 3.13 CI exposes runpy/loader incompatibility.

observations.problem: The default structural complexity profiles still include pre-subpackage flat ops/scripts paths such as ops/scripts/auto_improve_readiness_runtime.py, so the current report records zero metrics for moved modules and can miss the actual release/learning builder hot spots.
observations.why_it_matters: The long-term refactor plan depends on structural budget evidence; stale monitored paths make the budget look stale or noisy just when it should guide builder decomposition priorities.
observations.automation_candidate: Automated by refreshing DEFAULT_TARGET_PROFILES to canonical subpackage paths, adding a default-target existence regression test, and regenerating structural/function budget evidence.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. Future refactor slices can now rely on structural-complexity-budget to measure current canonical files before choosing builder decomposition targets.

observations.problem: The execution-lane policy already described test-artifact-finalization as unsafe in source-package extracts, but the Make target did not run the guard directly, so source package users could still see missing generated-artifact failures instead of a lane-boundary explanation.
observations.why_it_matters: Source packages intentionally exclude ops/reports and other full-vault generated evidence; a direct full-vault-only target should fail fast with the source-package alternatives rather than looking like a packaging defect.
observations.automation_candidate: Automated by adding a test-artifact-finalization-lane-guard Make target, narrowing policy semantics to block only source_package_extract for artifact-finalization, and extending execution_lane_guard with blocked_lanes support so public/partial CI remains allowed.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed for direct test-artifact-finalization execution. Future lane-boundary additions should prefer blocked_lanes when the problem is source-package misuse rather than an absolute full-vault requirement.

observations.surface: build_canonical_report_envelope source_paths across moved ops/scripts subpackages
observations.problem: Several report builders still declare pre-subpackage flat ops/scripts source paths even though the actual files now live under mechanism/core/eval/release/supply_chain subpackages; before this task the canonical report envelope accepted source_paths but did not fingerprint them.
observations.why_it_matters: Release and freshness evidence should fingerprint the actual owning files. Flat alias compatibility is useful for CLI imports, but source provenance needs a central source-path fingerprint so path drift changes producer inputs.
observations.automation_candidate: Automated by adding source_paths to canonical report envelope input_fingerprints, resolving unique flat ops/scripts/<module>.py aliases to their canonical subpackage files before hashing, and adding a regression test for alias equivalence and content-change sensitivity.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed for provenance safety in this task. A future cleanup can canonicalize remaining literal source_paths in individual builders or add diagnostics for ambiguous aliases, but shared envelope fingerprints now bind reports to the actual current source files.

observations.problem: Artifact finalization found canonical supply-chain reports that had generated_at and schemas but no canonical artifact envelope metadata, leaving freshness/currentness unable to prove the report graph was fully sealed.
observations.why_it_matters: Dependency reproducibility and SBOM/provenance evidence should be replayable from the same source and dependency fingerprints; envelope gaps make release evidence look current while omitting producer, input fingerprint, and currentness proof.
observations.automation_candidate: Automated by embedding canonical artifact envelopes in the supply-chain advisory/model/in-toto/sigstore/benchmark generators, extending their schemas to allow the embedded envelope metadata, and regenerating supply-chain artifacts plus freshness evidence.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. artifact-freshness now reports missing_artifact_envelope_count=0 and supply-chain reproducibility evidence is sealed by source_tree_fingerprint plus dependency_fingerprint.

observations.problem: The sealed release lane refreshed selector-free full-suite evidence with 1212 passing node ids, but the release guard still expected the older 1141 collect-only node count.
observations.why_it_matters: A stale full-suite count stops sealed release closeout after valid full-suite evidence has already passed, while also making future operators question whether the guard is enforcing current evidence or an old fixture.
observations.automation_candidate: Automated by updating TEST_EXECUTION_SUMMARY_FULL_EXPECTED_NODE_COUNT and the static/documentation contracts to the freshly generated 1212-node full-suite evidence.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. Future test additions or removals should continue to update the expected node count only after a refreshed selector-free full-suite run has passed and promoted ops/reports/test-execution-summary-full.json.

observations.problem: The full sealed closeout target finished by writing the sealed rehearsal check to tmp/release-closeout-sealed-rehearsal-check.json after operator-release-summary and post-seal attestation had already observed a clean tmp JSON state.
observations.why_it_matters: A release can appear sealed at target exit while a follow-up operator summary immediately degrades to sealed_release_status=blocked_tmp_json, making post-seal verification non-idempotent.
observations.automation_candidate: Automated by making the full sealed target write its final rehearsal check to a build/release sidecar while preserving the standalone sealed-check tmp output for diagnostics.
observations.suggested_followup: Closed in this task. Full sealed closeout now leaves the final rehearsal check at build/release/release-closeout-sealed-rehearsal-check.json and does not dirty tmp JSON hygiene after post-seal operator evidence.
